findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "fetch" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

labels.name: search_attorneys
labels.description:
Search petitioner attorneys and firms by name. Returns per-attorney
    aggregate stats: case count, compensation rate, main vaccines, and firm.
    Useful for 'who has handled X cases' questions.

labels.name: get_attorney
labels.description:
Get one attorney's profile: bio (when available), aggregate outcomes,
    and their most recent cases. Name is matched case-insensitively.

labels.name: get_special_master
labels.description:
Decision patterns for one special master: outcome breakdown, top
    vaccines/conditions in their docket, and recent cases.

labels.name: search
labels.description:
Search VICP vaccine-injury cases by free text. Returns result ids for
    use with fetch. (Alias of search_cases for deep-research clients; prefer
    search_cases when structured filters are available.)

labels.name: fetch
labels.description:
Fetch the full record + source text of one case by its id (package_id).
    (Deep-research companion to search.)

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "fetch" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "search_cases" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet:
before: "(hash 279c0672)" → after: "Search VICP cases with structured filters.

    - query: free text matched against petitioner identifier, condition,
      package id, caption name and the AI case summary.
    - vaccine: canonical vaccine name (use get_filters() for valid values,
      e.g. "Influenza (flu)", "DTaP/DPT", "MMR", "CO"

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.
